data.description:

B & D Strategic Holdings Limited ( (HK:1780) ) just unveiled an update.

Rongzun International Holdings Group Limited has requested a halt in trading of its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, effective from 9:00 a.m. on 8 April 2026. The company, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.

The trading suspension comes as the company prepares to release an announcement containing inside information, in connection with matters under the Hong Kong Code on Takeovers and Mergers. Trading will remain halted until the market is informed of the details, a move aimed at ensuring fair disclosure and preventing disorderly trading ahead of the pending announcement.

data.title: Congyu Intelligent Agricultural Clarifies Clerical Error in Share Option Grant
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Finance Investment Holdings Limited ( (HK:0875) ) has issued an update.

Congyu Intelligent Agricultural Holdings Limited issued a clarification regarding an earlier announcement on the grant of share options, correcting a clerical error in the figures allocated to an executive director and other eligible participants. The company specified that executive director and substantial shareholder Lin Yuhao received 54,613 options instead of 5,461,300, while other eligible participants, mainly employees, were granted 50,097,100 options, leaving the total number of options at 54,613,013 and confirming that all other details of the original announcement remain unchanged.

Following the correction, a total of 4,515,913 options were granted to directors, including 4,461,300 options to executive director Yang Yan, with the balance allocated to staff across the group. The clarification aims to ensure accurate disclosure of equity-based incentives, which is important for shareholders and investors monitoring potential dilution, management incentives, and corporate governance practices at the Hong Kong-listed agricultural group.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0875) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Finance Investment Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0875 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Finance Investment Holdings Limited

Congyu Intelligent Agricultural Holdings Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 875. The group operates in the agricultural sector, with a focus on intelligent and modern farming-related businesses through its subsidiaries and related entities.

data.title: KKCG Maritime’s Tender Offer for Ferretti Draws Limited Early Acceptance
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ferretti S.p.A. ( (IT:YACHT) ) has issued an announcement.

KKCG Maritime has launched a voluntary conditional partial public tender offer to acquire up to 52,132,861 shares of Ferretti S.p.A., equal to 15.4% of its share capital, but has so far received valid acceptances for only 354,036 shares, representing about 0.10% of Ferretti’s issued shares. The low take-up at this stage leaves KKCG Maritime’s stake only modestly above its existing 14.5% holding, and further updates on acceptances and final allocation will determine whether the offer meaningfully reshapes Ferretti’s shareholder base.

Immediately before the offer, KKCG Maritime already held 49,030,027 Ferretti shares and a related party held a small additional stake, with no other concert-party acquisitions or securities lending reported during the offer period. The outcome of the tender will clarify whether KKCG Maritime can significantly increase its influence over the luxury yacht maker or whether limited investor participation signals resistance to further concentration of ownership.

More about Ferretti S.p.A.

Ferretti S.p.A. is a yacht manufacturer listed in Hong Kong and identified on EXM as “YACHT,” operating in the luxury leisure marine sector. The company focuses on designing and producing high-end recreational vessels, targeting affluent global customers seeking premium yachting and maritime lifestyle products.

Leader Education Limited ( (HK:1449) ) has provided an update.

Leader Education Limited has issued a supplemental announcement detailing the circumstances and rationale behind its recent change of auditors, following the retirement of BDO Limited at the 24 February 2026 annual general meeting after a failure to agree on audit fees for the year ending 31 August 2026. After a competitive tender process, the board and audit committee selected Jon Gepsom CPA Limited, citing a more favorable fee range, efficiency from using PRC-based personnel and off-peak timing, and an audit approach that leverages digital tools without reducing scope or quality.

The audit committee stated it had assessed Jon Gepsom’s independence, competence and capability in line with regulatory guidance, noting no conflicts of interest, relevant industry experience, adequate manpower and resources, and adherence to professional ethics standards. The company emphasized that the new appointment aligns with its cost-control objectives while maintaining high-quality audits, signaling a focus on operational efficiency in its financial reporting and regulatory compliance framework.

data.title: Shanghai Bao Pharma Wins NMPA Nod for Recombinant Human Hyaluronidase Injection
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:2659) ) has shared an update.

Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals announced that its internally developed Human Hyaluronidase for Injection, under project code KJ017, has been approved for marketing by China’s National Medical Products Administration. The 385U/vial formulation is cleared as an adjuvant to subcutaneous infusion of solutions such as sodium chloride injection and sodium lactate Ringer’s injection, enabling large-volume subcutaneous administration.

KJ017 is a highly glycosylated recombinant human hyaluronidase that temporarily increases subcutaneous tissue permeability, offering a safer and more convenient alternative to traditional animal-derived hyaluronidase. By reducing immunogenicity risk, eliminating the need for pre-use skin testing, and improving batch consistency, the product could enhance clinical practice for difficult intravenous access patients and strengthen the company’s position in advanced injectable delivery technologies.

More about Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals Co Ltd Class H

Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. is a China-based drug manufacturer listed in Hong Kong that focuses on developing innovative injectable therapies. The company targets hospital and clinical settings with products designed to improve drug delivery, particularly for patients who face challenges with conventional intravenous administration routes.

China Ruyi Holdings Limited ( (HK:0136) ) has shared an update.

China Ruyi Holdings has signed a 2026 Game Cooperation Framework Agreement with Tencent Computer, under which the parties will cooperate in the gaming sector from the satisfaction of conditions precedent through 31 December 2028. Over the same term, a separate drama and movies framework will see China Ruyi license online broadcasting and distribution rights for its original series and films to Tencent-linked entities and co-produce made-to-order dramas.

Because Tencent, through its subsidiaries, holds about 15.37% of China Ruyi’s shares, these arrangements are classified as continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong listing rules and trigger stricter compliance requirements. The company will seek independent shareholders’ approval at a general meeting, with Tencent-related shareholders abstaining, and has appointed an independent board committee and financial adviser, signaling the strategic importance and regulatory sensitivity of the expanded partnership with Tencent.

CK Infrastructure Holdings has called a special general meeting for 27 April 2026 in Hong Kong, to be held as a hybrid physical and online event, giving shareholders the option to attend and vote via an online platform or in person. An announcement from CK Hutchison Holdings ( (HK:0001) ) is now available.

CK Hutchison Holdings has called an extraordinary general meeting for 27 April 2026 in Hong Kong, to be held in hybrid format with both physical attendance and online participation via a dedicated platform, allowing shareholders to vote and raise questions remotely or in person. The meeting will consider an ordinary resolution seeking shareholder approval for a disposal transaction under a share purchase agreement involving the sale of certain shares and shareholder debt instruments held by a subsidiary, and will authorise directors to take all necessary steps to implement the deal.

The company is encouraging shareholders who do not attend in person or online to appoint the meeting chairman as their proxy and submit proxy forms ahead of the deadline, with completed proxies remaining revocable if shareholders later decide to participate directly. The poll-based voting structure and hybrid arrangements underscore CK Hutchison’s efforts to facilitate broad shareholder engagement and ensure procedural compliance as it seeks consent for a significant portfolio adjustment within its infrastructure-related investments, though detailed financial terms are contained in the accompanying circular rather than this notice.

Tianjin Tianbao Energy Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1671) ) has provided an announcement.

Tianjin Tianbao Energy announced that executive director Wang Geng has tendered his resignation due to job rearrangements, effective once shareholders approve a new executive director at the 2025 annual general meeting in 2026. The board noted that Wang has no disagreements with the company or claims against it, and expressed appreciation for his contributions, signalling an orderly leadership transition.

The board has nominated current chief financial officer and senior accountant Yin Ning as an executive director, with her term to run from shareholder approval at the AGM until the end of the fourth board session. Yin, who has held a series of senior finance and audit roles across Tianjin Tianbao group entities since 1993, will not receive additional director fees beyond her existing CFO remuneration, underscoring continuity in financial management and stable governance for stakeholders.

Power Assets Holdings ( (HK:0006) ) has provided an update.

Power Assets Holdings has convened a hybrid general meeting for 27 April 2026 in Hong Kong and online, seeking shareholder approval for a connected and major transaction tied to a share purchase agreement signed on 25 February 2026. The resolution covers the disposal of certain subsidiary shareholdings and shareholder debt instruments as part of a broader asset disposal, with directors to be authorised to execute all necessary steps, underscoring an ongoing reshaping of the group’s investment portfolio and capital allocation.

The meeting will be conducted via both physical attendance and an online platform, with voting to be taken by poll and shareholders able to appoint proxies or participate remotely, reflecting a continued emphasis on flexible corporate governance arrangements. Shareholders must meet specified record and registration dates to vote, and the company has outlined contingency plans for severe weather, signalling an effort to ensure procedural robustness and inclusive participation in a transaction that may influence its future asset mix and earnings profile.
